title: Open quantum dynamics without Complete Positivity: a criticism
authors: Fabio Benatti, Dariusz Chruściński, Saverio Pascazio
abstract: The requirement of complete positivity is very often regarded as a fundamental consistency condition for the description of open quantum dynamics. We critically examine this requirement and discuss both its physical motivations and its limitations. We analyze proposals based on restricting the domain of non-completely positive maps to subsets of compatible initial states. Using isotropic states as a concrete example, we show that such domain restrictions become increasingly severe with growing system dimension, revealing an intrinsic weakness of the compatibility-based approach.
summary: The paper investigates whether one can describe open quantum systems using maps that are positive but not completely positive by restricting the allowed initial states. It proves that this 'compatibility' approach fails in high dimensions because the set of allowed entangled states shrinks to zero, suggesting that Complete Positivity is a necessary requirement for consistent high-dimensional dynamics.
main_problem: The paper critiques the validity of abandoning the requirement of Complete Positivity (CP) in open quantum dynamics by using a 'compatibility-based' approach that restricts initial states.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that as the system dimension increases, the volume of compatible entangled states vanishes, revealing an intrinsic weakness in non-CP dynamical models.
method: The authors use spectral analysis of the lifted map and volume calculations of the parameter interval for isotropic states to evaluate the compatibility of non-CP maps.
model_or_system: The study focuses on finite d-level quantum systems interacting with an environment, specifically analyzing a family of positive but non-CP maps (Lambda_mu) and isotropic qudit states.
key_observables: The volume of compatible entangled states (V_comp) and the fidelity (F) of isotropic states.
important_parameters: System dimension (d), map parameter (mu), and state fidelity (F).
main_assumptions: The analysis is primarily focused on finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces and specific families of channels and states.
paper_structure: The paper begins by framing the dilemma of CP vs. non-CP dynamics, evaluates the pros and cons of the CP requirement, analyzes the 'compatibility-based' strategy using isotropic states, and concludes with the scaling of compatible state volumes in high dimensions.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for researchers in open quantum systems and many-body dynamics as it addresses a fundamental consistency condition (CP) and shows that non-CP models may be physically inconsistent with large-scale entanglement.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
